title: TrustContextTokenClaims
description: AITP v0.2 Trust Context Token — decoded JWS payload claims (RFC-AITP-0005). On the wire a TCT is an opaque compact JWS string (typ 'aitp-tct+jwt', RFC-AITP-0001 §5.4.5); this schema validates the decoded claims object. The compact-string form is pinned by the $defs/CompactJws pattern, referenced by embedding schemas.

CompactJws.description: RFC 7515 compact JWS: exactly three non-empty unpadded-base64url segments. Strict parsing per RFC-AITP-0001 §5.4.5.

Aid.description: Agent ID per RFC-AITP-0001 §5.3: legacy Ed25519 form, algorithm-tagged Ed25519 form, or P-256 form.

Cnf.description: RFC 7800 confirmation claim, jkt form only (RFC-AITP-0001 §5.4.4).

jkt.description: RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint (SHA-256, unpadded base64url) of the bound public key. MUST match the key encoded in the token's sub AID.

ver.description: Protocol version private claim. MUST be 'aitp/0.2'.

jti.description: UUID v4 unique token identifier. The revocation handle (RFC-AITP-0008).

sub.description: AID of the agent this TCT was issued for (the subject peer).

aud.description: AID of the intended consuming peer. MUST equal sub for peer-issued TCTs. Single string, never an array.

exp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) of expiry. Consumers MUST enforce this, and exp MUST NOT exceed the issuer Manifest's expires_at (RFC-AITP-0005 §10.4).

grants.description: Capability strings granted to the subject. Empty-grants TCTs are forbidden (RFC-AITP-0004 §4.1). Private claim.

ext.description: Optional extension namespace per RFC-AITP-0001 §5.4.5 and RFC-AITP-0012. Unknown keys inside ext MUST be ignored; unknown claims outside it are rejected by this schema.
